articles.title: Lemonade Stock Upgrade: Autonomous Vehicle Insurance Initiative with Tesla - News and Statistics
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Lemonade stock to overweight, citing its partnership with Tesla for self-driving car insurance. This collaboration allows Lemonade access to Tesla's driving data, positioning the insurer at the forefront of evolving auto insurance models for autonomous vehicles. Lemonade had previously launched a product for autonomous vehicles offering discounts based on self-driving engagement, anticipating reduced accident rates.
articles.text_to_analyze: Lemonade Stock Upgrade: Autonomous Vehicle Insurance Initiative with Tesla - News and Statistics. articles.title: Lemonade (LMND) Climbs 15.8% on Morgan Stanley Rating, Price Target Hike
articles.summary: Lemonade Inc. (LMND) saw its stock price surge by 15.8% after Morgan Stanley upgraded its rating to "overweight" and increased its price target to $85. This optimism stems from Lemonade's first-mover advantage in offering car insurance for autonomous vehicles, specifically Tesla's full self-driving cars, which includes significant per-mile rate reductions for FSD-engaged driving. Morgan Stanley believes this positions Lemonade for further growth as autonomous technology advances and provides more savings for customers.

articles.title: Lemonade (LMND) Stock Soars 17% Following Morgan Stanley Upgrade
articles.summary: Lemonade (LMND) shares surged 17% after Morgan Stanley upgraded the stock to Overweight, setting a price target of $85. The upgrade was driven by Lemonade's strategic advantage in autonomous driving insurance, particularly its partnership with Tesla (TSLA) which provides unique access to vehicle data for enhanced risk assessment. While the company shows strong revenue growth, it faces challenges in profitability and has a speculative valuation with high growth expectations.

articles.title: [424B2] GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC Prospectus Supplement
articles.summary: GS Finance Corp. is offering $250,000 in Autocallable Variable Coupon Equity-Linked Notes due 2031, guaranteed by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. These notes are tied to the performance of Class A common stock of Palantir Technologies, and the common stocks of Tesla, NVIDIA, and Broadcom. The notes feature monthly variable coupons and an automatic call option if the underlying stocks meet specific price thresholds.

articles.title: Samsung Elec plans to produce Tesla chips starting late 2027
articles.summary: Samsung Electronics announced its expectation to begin volume production of chips for Tesla at its Texas factory in the second half of 2027. This information was shared by Han Jin-man, Samsung Electronics President and Head of Foundry Business, during a shareholders' meeting. This move signifies a significant supply chain development for both companies.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley upgrades Lemonade stock on Tesla partnership potential
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley upgraded Lemonade Inc. (NYSE:LMND) to Overweight from Equalweight, raising its price target to $85, citing the company's partnership with Tesla for auto insurance. The firm believes this partnership gives Lemonade a first-mover advantage and could lead to tenfold growth in its autonomous vehicle insurance exposure. Despite current valuation concerns, Lemonade recently exceeded Q4 2025 earnings expectations with a narrower loss per share and higher revenue.

Despite current valuation concerns, Lemonade recently exceeded Q4 2025 earnings expectations with a narrower loss per share and higher revenue.

articles.title: While Tesla Fights in Court, Its Competitors Are Quietly Winning the Autonomy War
articles.summary: Tesla is facing legal battles in California over its "Autopilot" and "Full Self-Driving" (FSD) claims, with regulators asserting the company has misled consumers about its vehicles' autonomous capabilities. Despite rebranding its FSD subscription to "(Supervised)," Tesla is suing to reverse rulings that deemed its marketing false, even as its Level 2 autonomy system has been linked to numerous fatalities. Meanwhile, competitors like BYD are making strides towards true full autonomy while Tesla grapples with the realities of its deficient technology.

Meanwhile, competitors like BYD are making strides towards true full autonomy while Tesla grapples with the realities of its deficient technology.

articles.title: Why Is Lemonade (LMND) Stock Rocketing Higher Today
articles.summary: Lemonade (LMND) stock surged over 16% after Morgan Stanley upgraded it to Overweight with an $85 price target, citing its partnership with Tesla for auto insurance and a "first-mover advantage." The upgrade followed strong fourth-quarter financial results, where Lemonade beat revenue projections, narrowed its net loss, and provided optimistic 2026 guidance. Despite the recent gains, Lemonade's stock has been volatile and is still down over 11% year-to-date and 30% below its 52-week high.

articles.title: Lemonade stock jumps after Morgan Stanley upgrades to Overweight on Tesla partnership (LMND:NYSE)
articles.summary: Lemonade (LMND) stock jumped 17% after Morgan Stanley upgraded the company to Overweight from Equalweight. The upgrade was attributed to Lemonade's first-mover advantage in autonomous driving and its strategic partnership with Tesla (TSLA), which offers significant technical collaboration benefits.

articles.title: A French beverage company could derail Tesla’s ‘Cybercab’ name
articles.summary: Tesla's plans for its "Cybercab" robotaxi service are facing a hurdle from an unexpected source: a French beverage wholesaler called UniBev. UniBev beat Tesla to the trademark filing for Cybercab, despite its owner appearing to be a Tesla fan. Tesla is now challenging the trademark claim, which could delay the launch of its robotaxis until 2027 if the issue goes to trial.
